proposed_hypotheses.text: Performance-status-like ordinal feature feature_014 (values 0/1/2) is inversely associated with pfs_months: each one-unit increase shortens mean pfs_months.
Squamous histology (feature_006 == 'squamous') is associated with shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma.
Current-smoker status (feature_001 == 'current') is associated with shorter pfs_months than former or never smokers.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate OLS slope of pfs_months on feature_014 = -1.099 months per unit (p<1e-300). Group means: 4.33 (0), 3.18 (1), 2.15 (2). Strong monotone inverse relationship consistent with ECOG-like performance status.
Mean pfs_months: squamous 2.81 vs adenocarcinoma 3.67. Welch t = -39.5, p~0. Squamous histology shortens PFS by ~0.86 months on average.
Group means: current 2.99, former 3.61, never 3.68. ANOVA F=433, p=3.7e-187. Current smokers have ~0.6 month shorter PFS than former/never; former vs never essentially equal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 (binary, ~65% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months and likely encodes an unfavorable disease-extent indicator (e.g., advanced stage / metastatic disease).
feature_011 (binary, ~25% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_028 (binary, rare ~5% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months and may represent a poor-prognosis site of disease (e.g., CNS involvement).

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 4.36 (feature_023=0) vs 2.93 (feature_023=1). Welch t = -83, p~0. Diff = -1.44 months.
Mean pfs_months 3.66 (0) vs 2.74 (1). p~0. Diff = -0.92 months.
Mean pfs_months 3.47 (0) vs 2.66 (1). p=5.6e-66. feature_028=1 (5% prevalence) shortens PFS by 0.81 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016 (binary, ~13% prevalence) is associated with LONGER pfs_months and may represent a favorable molecular biomarker.
feature_018 (binary, ~35% prevalence) is associated with LONGER pfs_months and may be a treatment indicator.
feature_022 (binary, ~13% prevalence) is mildly associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 3.33 (0) vs 4.11 (1). p=7e-94. feature_016=1 lengthens PFS by 0.78 months on average.
Mean pfs_months 3.32 (0) vs 3.64 (1). p=5.6e-49. feature_018=1 associated with 0.32 months longer PFS marginally.
Mean pfs_months 3.41 (0) vs 3.60 (1). p=6.5e-11. Small +0.19 month effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_015 (range 30-90, median 65) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
Continuous feature_017 (range 0-28, median 3) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
Continuous feature_025 (range 1.7-5.5, median 3.8 - resembling serum albumin) is positively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.spearmanr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = +0.769, p~0. Univariate OLS slope = +0.170 months per unit. feature_015 is by far the strongest single prognostic continuous variable in the dataset.
Spearman rho = -0.125, p=2e-172. Univariate OLS slope = -0.073 per unit. Higher feature_017 predicts shorter PFS (consistent with a tumor-burden / inflammatory marker).
Spearman rho = +0.100, p=6e-111. Univariate OLS slope = +0.441 per unit. Higher feature_025 predicts longer PFS (consistent with serum albumin, a known prognostic marker).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_031 (binary, ~45% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months independent of histology and smoking status.

Other continuous lab features (feature_032, feature_024, feature_010, feature_003, feature_030, feature_008, feature_004, feature_013, feature_026, feature_029, feature_007, feature_033, feature_009) have negligible univariate association with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 3.52 (feature_031=0) vs 3.32 (1). p=7.7e-25. Diff = -0.20 months. Distribution is balanced across histology, smoking, and feature_016, so the marginal effect is not confounded by these.

analyses.code: for c in ['feature_032','feature_024','feature_010','feature_003','feature_030','feature_008','feature_004','feature_013','feature_026','feature_029','feature_007','feature_033','feature_009']: print(c, stats.spearmanr(df[c], df['pfs_months']))
analyses.result_summary: All thirteen continuous laboratory-style features have |Spearman rho| < 0.015. Strongest is feature_032 (rho = -0.014, p=0.002); the rest are not significant. These features are essentially noise predictors of pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model on pfs_months, the dominant prognostic features are feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, squamous histology, current-smoker status, feature_016, and feature_018; together they should explain a large fraction of the outcome variance.

analyses.code: import statsmodels.api as sm; X=sm.add_constant(df_full[predictors]); sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'],X).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted multivariable OLS with all 33 features yields R^2 = 0.894. Largest coefficients (months): feature_015 +0.168 per unit, feature_014 -1.10 per level, feature_023 -1.42, feature_011 -0.91, squamous -0.72, current smoker -0.55, feature_016 +0.89, feature_018 +0.32. feature_012/_020/_027 (each ~30-50% prevalence) are non-significant (|coef|<0.01). Most laboratory continuous features are non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the four high-prevalence binary features that look treatment-like (feature_012 ~50%, feature_018 ~35%, feature_020 ~30%, feature_027 ~30%), only feature_018 has a non-zero average effect on pfs_months; feature_012, feature_020, feature_027 have null marginal effects.

analyses.result_summary: Marginal mean PFS by treatment-candidate: feature_012 (3.449 vs 3.414, p=0.08), feature_020 (3.439 vs 3.414, p=0.26), feature_027 (3.431 vs 3.434, p=0.87) - all null. feature_018 (3.319 vs 3.641, p=5.6e-49) - the only one with a clear marginal positive effect. Hypothesis supported: feature_018 is the only one with marginal main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The marginal pfs_months benefit of feature_018 is heterogeneous: there exists at least one binary feature whose interaction with feature_018 is highly significant in an adjusted OLS model.

analyses.result_summary: Treatment-by-feature interaction screen for feature_018 (adjusted for the major prognostic features). Largest interaction: feature_018 x feature_016 with coefficient +2.535 (p~0). Next: feature_018 x feature_031 = -0.585 (p~0). Then feature_018 x feature_028 = -0.343 (p=2e-27). Smaller: x smoke_never -0.327, x smoke_current +0.253, x feature_005 -0.328, x feature_022 -0.208. Hypothesis strongly supported: feature_018 has substantial heterogeneity, dominated by one biomarker (feature_016).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018's effect on pfs_months is concentrated in feature_016=1 patients: the pfs benefit of feature_018 is approximately +2.5 months in feature_016=1 and approximately 0 in feature_016=0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_016=0: 3.334 (tx=0) vs 3.329 (tx=1) - essentially zero effect. feature_016=1: 3.213 (tx=0) vs 5.766 (tx=1) - +2.55 months. Difference-in-differences = +2.55, t-test for the within-feature_016=1 effect p~0. Hypothesis confirmed: the marginal benefit of feature_018 across the whole cohort is entirely driven by the ~13% of patients with feature_016=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_019 (range 0-0.8, median 0.30 - resembling PD-L1 expression) does NOT modify the effect of feature_018 on pfs_months once feature_016 status is accounted for.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS interaction coefficient feature_018 x feature_019 = +0.015 (p=0.64). Stratified analysis: feature_018 effect within feature_019 quartiles is +0.38, +0.27, +0.34, +0.30 - flat across quartiles. feature_019 is not a treatment-effect modifier for feature_018, suggesting feature_018 is NOT an immunotherapy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_016=1 patients, feature_028=1 abolishes the pfs_months benefit of feature_018: the within-stratum tx effect is +2.7 in feature_028=0 vs near 0 (or negative) in feature_028=1.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1 (n=6368): feature_028=0 stratum: 3.247 (tx=0) vs 5.914 (tx=1) - +2.667. feature_028=1 stratum: 2.434 (tx=0) vs 2.084 (tx=1) - -0.349 (n=87 treated). Triple interaction feature_018 x feature_016 x feature_028 in adjusted OLS: coef = -2.62, p=3e-130. Hypothesis confirmed: feature_028 is a hard 'resistance' modifier that wipes out the benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_016=1 patients, feature_031=1 also abolishes the pfs_months benefit of feature_018, independently of feature_028.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1 (n=6368): feature_031=0 stratum: 3.232 (tx=0) vs 7.876 (tx=1) - +4.644 months. feature_031=1 stratum: 3.191 (tx=0) vs 3.179 (tx=1) - -0.012 months. Stratified interaction p~0. Hypothesis confirmed: feature_031=1 also fully nullifies the benefit, independently of and as strongly as feature_028.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete subgroup that responds to feature_018 is feature_016=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_031=0; in this 3-predicate subgroup the mean pfs_months gain is approximately +4.8 months. Outside this subgroup the average treatment effect is approximately zero.

analyses.result_summary: Active subgroup n=3370 (6.7% of cohort). Treated n=1191 mean pfs=8.089; untreated n=2179 mean pfs=3.257. Effect = +4.832 months, Welch t=58.3, p~0. Outside the subgroup (n=46630): tx effect = -0.006 months, p=0.75. Hypothesis fully supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A full 3-way interaction OLS model {feature_018, feature_016, feature_028, feature_031} confirms that the only large positive interaction is feature_018 x feature_016 (~+4.7 months), and that this benefit is cancelled by feature_018 x feature_016 x feature_028 (~-2.5) and feature_018 x feature_016 x feature_031 (~-4.6).

analyses.code: X with main, 2-way, 3-way terms across feature_018, feature_016, feature_028, feature_031 + covariates
analyses.result_summary: Coefficients (months, adjusted for feature_015, feature_023, feature_011, feature_014, feature_017, feature_025): feature_018 main = -0.004 (ns); feature_018 x feature_016 = +4.680 (p~0); feature_018 x feature_028 = -0.010 (ns); feature_018 x feature_031 = +0.015 (ns); feature_018 x feature_016 x feature_028 = -2.518 (p=3e-201); feature_018 x feature_016 x feature_031 = -4.613 (p~0). Direct interpretation: there is no benefit anywhere except feature_016=1, and within feature_016=1 the benefit is removed if either feature_028=1 or feature_031=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The other treatment-candidate features feature_012, feature_020, feature_027 have no meaningful treatment-effect heterogeneity on pfs_months: in interaction screens against the major prognostic and biomarker features they yield no interactions with |coef|>0.06 at p<1e-4.

analyses.code:
for tx in ['feature_012','feature_020','feature_027']:
    for mod in modifiers: ... sm.OLS().fit()
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions found (none meaningful): feature_012 x feature_016 = -0.054 (p=0.007), feature_012 x feature_023 = +0.031 (p=0.024), feature_020 x feature_016 = -0.024 (p=0.26), feature_027 x feature_011 = +0.030 (p=0.07). All effect sizes are tiny vs the 4.7 month effect for feature_018, and none survive multiple-comparison correction. Hypothesis supported: these three are not active treatments (they may be unrelated baseline covariates).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the active subgroup (feature_016=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_031=0), histology (squamous vs adenocarcinoma) does NOT meaningfully modify the magnitude of the feature_018 treatment effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Within the active subgroup, stratifying further by histology gives essentially the same magnitude of feature_018 benefit in adenocarcinoma and squamous (~+4.5 to +5 months in both). The feature_018 x squamous interaction within the wider feature_016=1 group is +0.108 with p=1.4e-12 - statistically detectable but quantitatively trivial vs the 4.7-month main interaction. Hypothesis supported: histology does not meaningfully modify the response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_016=1 group, smoking status modifies the feature_018 effect only modestly; the benefit remains substantial across current/former/never smokers.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1: feature_018 effect by smoking - current 2.88->5.36 (+2.48); former 3.49->6.15 (+2.66); never 3.60->5.36 (+1.76, n=46 treated). The smoke_never x feature_018 interaction in the full-cohort adjusted model is -0.33 (p=1e-65), partly an artifact of small n in the never+feature_016=1 cell. Hypothesis supported: feature_018 helps across smoking strata when feature_016=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_016=1 group, performance-status-like feature_014 does NOT meaningfully modify the feature_018 effect on pfs_months: the benefit is similar across feature_014 levels.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1, feature_018 incremental effect by feature_014: similar magnitude across levels (interaction p in stratified OLS = 0.62 inside feature_016=1). No meaningful modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_015 (the dominant prognostic continuous feature) is approximately additive with the feature_018 x feature_016 effect: it contributes roughly +0.17 months per unit in both treated and untreated patients in the active subgroup, without a strong multiplicative interaction.

analyses.code: OLS with feature_018, feature_015, and their interaction within feature_016=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_031=0
analyses.result_summary: Within the active subgroup, OLS slopes on feature_015: untreated +0.165 per unit, treated +0.170 per unit; interaction coefficient ~+0.005 (ns). Hypothesis supported: feature_015 acts as an additive prognostic factor without modifying the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A T-learner using gradient boosting to estimate conditional average treatment effect (CATE) of feature_018 on pfs_months will identify the same active subgroup: the highest-CATE patients are dominantly feature_016=1 and feature_031=0.

analyses.code: GradientBoostingRegressor on feature_018=0 and =1 separately; CATE = m1.predict - m0.predict
analyses.result_summary: Patients with estimated CATE > 2 months (n=3307): 100% have feature_016=1, 0% have feature_031=1, 0.6% have feature_028=1. A regression tree on CATE places the first split at feature_016 (CATE jumps from ~0 to >3 months at feature_016=1) and the second split at feature_031 (CATE 4-5 if feature_031=0, ~0 if feature_031=1). Hypothesis confirmed; the data-driven CATE-tree exactly recovers the algebraic subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 (~13% prevalence, very small +0.19 marginal effect) is NOT a treatment with substantial heterogeneity: its largest interaction in an adjusted screen is with feature_031 (+0.137, p=4e-12) and is quantitatively far smaller than the feature_018 x feature_016 effect.

analyses.code: treatment-by-modifier screen with feature_022 as putative tx
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions for feature_022: x feature_031 = +0.137 (p=4e-12), x feature_021 = -0.053 (p=0.04), x feature_005 = +0.101 (p=0.08). All effect sizes <0.2 months. feature_022 looks like a small-effect baseline characteristic rather than an active treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028 acts as a poor-prognosis marker AND a feature_018 treatment-effect modifier: a single subgroup definition feature_016=1 AND feature_028=1 has worse PFS and no benefit from feature_018.

analyses.result_summary: feature_016=1 & feature_028=1 stratum (n=259): tx=0 mean PFS 2.43 (n=172); tx=1 mean PFS 2.08 (n=87). Treatment effect = -0.35 months, t-test p=0.30. Hypothesis confirmed: feature_028 marks a more aggressive disease subset where the targetable benefit of feature_018 disappears (and trends slightly negative).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the cohort, the major adverse prognostic features (feature_023, feature_011, feature_014, feature_028, feature_005, current smoker, squamous, feature_017, feature_032) act approximately additively on pfs_months and do not have substantial interactions with each other beyond the feature_018-related effects already characterised.

analyses.code: Pairwise interaction screen on the 9 adverse features in adjusted OLS
analyses.result_summary: An exhaustive pairwise-interaction screen across the listed 9 prognostic features yields no |coef|>0.15 with p<1e-10 once main effects are included. The model's R^2 increases from 0.894 (additive) to 0.896 with all 36 pairwise interactions. The treatment-effect heterogeneity in the dataset is concentrated in the feature_018 x feature_016 x {feature_028, feature_031} structure described in iterations 11-14; otherwise the prognostic structure is essentially additive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When the active-subgroup interaction term (feature_018 * feature_016 * (1-feature_028) * (1-feature_031)) is added to the additive multivariable OLS model, it explains essentially all of the treatment-related variance and the simple feature_018 main effect becomes 0.

analyses.result_summary: Adding the indicator feature_018 x I(feature_016=1, feature_028=0, feature_031=0) to the additive model: coefficient = +4.80 months (p~0); the marginal feature_018 main effect drops to ~0; model R^2 increases from 0.894 to ~0.93. This single subgroup-treatment term replaces the feature_018 main and all its component interactions.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: For the outcome pfs_months, feature_018 (the only treatment with detectable effect heterogeneity) increases pfs_months by approximately +4.8 months only in the subgroup defined by (feature_016 == 1) AND (feature_028 == 0) AND (feature_031 == 0); outside this subgroup the average treatment effect of feature_018 is null. Other apparent treatment candidates (feature_012, feature_020, feature_027) have no detectable effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: sub_active = df[(df['feature_016']==1)&(df['feature_028']==0)&(df['feature_031']==0)]; ttest_ind(sub_active.loc[sub_active['feature_018']==1,'pfs_months'], sub_active.loc[sub_active['feature_018']==0,'pfs_months']); inv = df[~mask]; ttest_ind(inv tx vs ctrl)
analyses.result_summary: Active subgroup (n=3370, 6.7% of cohort): tx=1 mean PFS 8.089 vs tx=0 mean PFS 3.257; effect +4.832 months, p~0. Complement (n=46630): tx effect -0.006 months, p=0.75. The full subgroup specification - including feature_028 and feature_031 as 'unfavorable resistance modifiers' that suppress the targetable benefit - is required to capture the heterogeneity. feature_012, feature_020, feature_027: no effect (|coef|<0.04, all p>0.05 marginally; no meaningful interactions found).
